_instructions.STEP 1 — Someone submits the form on you: You get an email at nitin.edu476@gmail.com with their name, role, and message.
_instructions.STEP 2 — You decide to approve it: Copy the example entry from '_example' below into the 'testimonials' array. Fill in their details.
_instructions.STEP 3 — Set approved to true: Change 'approved' from false to true. If you want to hide it temporarily, set it to false.
_instructions.STEP 4 — Save and push: Run: git add testimonials.json && git commit -m 'add testimonial' && git push origin master
_instructions.STEP 5 — It appears live: GitHub Actions deploys in ~2 minutes. The card shows up on your site automatically.
_instructions.TO REJECT: Simply don't add the entry. Or add it with 'approved': false — it won't show on the site.

FIELDS EXPLAINED.name: Person's full name — e.g. 'Rahul K.'
FIELDS EXPLAINED.role: Their title and company — e.g. 'Senior Engineer · CARS24'
FIELDS EXPLAINED.text: Their message — do NOT add quotes, the site adds them automatically
FIELDS EXPLAINED.approved: true = show on site | false = hide from site

_example.name: Rahul K.
_example.text: Paste their exact message here. No need to add quotes.
